projects.bet: Compliance is painful, recurring, and regulated — and my supply-chain background makes this a domain play, not a random SaaS bet.
projects.notes: The clearest commercial path in the portfolio. Real modules are shipping; the open question is conversion, not capability. Get beta users to paid before adding verticals.

projects.bet: Serious keeper leagues are underserved by the big fantasy platforms — go deep for the hardcore, not wide for casuals.
projects.notes: Live and in-season, which is the hard part. Value now comes from accuracy and daily polish, not new features. Watch that in-season engagement holds.

projects.bet: Can a disciplined LLM make consistent trading calls? A learning experiment with real guardrails, not a product yet.
projects.notes: Deliberately paper-only. The value is the discipline and the harness, not the P&L. Don't let it graduate to real money until the gate criteria are actually met.

projects.bet: Replace clipboards and carbon copies at KCBS BBQ competitions with something judges actually prefer.
projects.notes: Blocked on external validation, not code — it needs real organizers to test before more building. The right next move is stakeholder time, not more features.

projects.bet: Hard-to-get Taipei reservations, turned into a marketplace with a real storefront.
projects.notes: Furthest from a working loop. Prove one listing-to-purchase flow end to end before anything else — breadth is premature until that exists.

projects.bet: A social layer on dining discovery — who you trust, not just star ratings.
projects.notes: Backend is live but the clients are paused, so it's effectively stalled. Decide whether to reignite the apps or let it rest — don't leave it half-lit.

projects.bet: Eighteen years of LA and SGV food coverage as a durable authority and a mapped archive.
projects.notes: The steady long-term asset — a compounding bet, not a growth one. Finish the venue tagging and the redesign, then let it run.

projects.bet: Home karaoke that just works — phones as remotes, no fuss.
projects.notes: Blocked on reliability: pairing has to be friend-proof before it's worth showing anyone. Nail the core connection before adding song features.

projects.bet: A living portfolio that proves the AI-assisted-operator thesis by simply existing and updating itself.
projects.notes: Doing its job. The meta-project — every feature here is also a demonstration. Keep it current; resist over-building.

projects.bet: Students share the change they want to make, mentors vouch for them, supporters back them — trust and safety first, built for minors.
projects.notes: Earliest stage and the most values-driven. Pre-launch; the gating work is trust, safety, and real payments, not features. Move carefully given the audience.

projects.bet: Leaders rehearse difficult conversations against an AI counterpart, then get a coached debrief on how they handled it.
projects.notes: The real product is the coaching debrief, not the chat. Prove the voice experience feels real before building UI around it — right sequencing.
